channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Eva Gabor's Hollywood Career and Style in the 1950s
item.description: Eva Gabor, born on February 11, 1919, in Hungary, became a celebrated Hungarian-American actress and socialite. During the 1950s, she established herself as a versatile performer in Hollywood and on B...

item.title: Worker Dies After Crane Collapse at Mindoro Bridge Construction Site
item.description: In Abra de Ilog, Occidental Mindoro, a tragic accident occurred on Tuesday afternoon when a crane collapsed at an ongoing bridge construction site, resulting in the death of a 61-year-old worker. Acco...

item.title: Khuzdar By-Election Postponed Over Security Concerns, Balochistan Govt Advises ECP
item.description: The Balochistan government has informed the Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) that holding the by-election for NA-256 Khuzdar is currently not feasible because of security concerns. The chief secr...

item.title: Perth mother runs for retinoblastoma awareness after son’s rare eye cancer diagnosis
item.description: A Perth mother, Emily Cross, has been thrust into a challenging journey after noticing a white reflection in her one-year-old son Peter’s eye. What initially seemed like a minor concern turned out to ...

item.title: Wickham Hosts Annual 750-Year-Old Horse Fair as Travellers Descend on Village
item.description: The Hampshire village of Wickham has once again become the centre of attention as hundreds of Gypsy and Roma travellers arrived for the annual Wickham Horse Fair, a tradition dating back 750 years. Th...

item.title: Desmond Elliot Pulls Out of Surulere APC Primary Citing Intimidation
item.description: Hon. Desmond Elliot wey don serve three terms as lawmaker for Surulere Constituency I for Lagos State House of Assembly don announce say e dey withdraw from the APC primary election for the same seat....

item.title: Joburg Mayor Awaits Investigation into Allegations Against MMC Mnisi
item.description: Johannesburg Mayor Dada Morero has stated that he is awaiting a formal report regarding allegations that Economic Development MMC Nomoya Mnisi and her bodyguard kidnapped a 16-year-old over the past w...

item.title: TANESCO expects Chalinze to Dodoma 400kV Power Line to be ready by August 2026
item.description: Tanzania Electric Supply Company (TANESCO) has confirmed that the construction of the Chalinze–Dodoma 400kV power line is progressing well and is expected to be fully completed by August 2026. This ma...

item.title: Kenyan Professor George Njoroge Receives KSh 446 Million Award for Pioneering Cancer Research
item.description: Professor George Njoroge, a renowned Kenyan researcher, has been awarded a prestigious KSh 446 million global prize alongside UK scientist Professor Robert Bristow for their groundbreaking work in ear...

item.title: Filipino-American Bowler Family Portrait from the 1920s During U.S. Occupation of the Philippines
item.description: This historical family portrait from around 1920 captures William Leslie Bowler, an American, alongside his Filipina wife, Dolores Alcantara, and several of their children during the period of U.S. co...

item.title: Trump states Iran nuclear talks approaching final phase
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has indicated that negotiations with Iran are nearing their final stage. Speaking on the matter, Trump suggested that progress has been made and that a potential resol...

item.title: Cher in the 1960s: Early Career, Style, and Rise to Fame
item.description: Before becoming a global pop icon and Academy Award winner, Cher, born Cheryl Sarkisian on May 20, 1946, emerged as a defining figure of 1960s counterculture. At just 16, she met Sonny Bono in Los Ang...

item.title: Bandit Attacks in Oyo Schools Leave Communities in Fear and Families Mourning
item.description: Nollywood actor Femi Adebayo don talk say the palava wey dey happen for parts of Oyo State don pain plenty people. Bandits wey dey ride motorbikes attack two schools at the same time – Baptist Nursery...

item.title: Study Highlights Rapid Spread of AI-Generated Antisemitism on Social Media
item.description: A recent report from CyberWell, an independent Israel-based non-profit focused on combating online antisemitism, reveals a concerning surge of AI-generated antisemitic content on major social media pl...

item.title: Véron Mosengo-Omba Elected President of Democratic Republic of Congo Football Federation
item.description: In a significant development for Congolese football, Véron Mosengo-Omba, who previously served as the General Secretary of the Confederation of African Football (CAF), has been elected as the new pres...

item.title: Prince Rahim Aga Khan V Meets President Zardari, Reaffirms AKDN Support for Pakistan Development
item.description: Prince Rahim al-Hussaini Aga Khan V, the new spiritual leader of the Ismaili community, arrived in Pakistan on May 20, 2026, for his first visit since taking on the role last year. He was warmly recei...

item.title: WHO assesses Ebola outbreak risk high in DRC and neighbouring countries, low worldwide
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has stated that the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) poses a high risk at national and regional levels, but remains low globally. So far, 51 cas...

item.title: Community restores the River Mease through wetland creation and farmer collaboration
item.description: A 13-year restoration project on the River Mease in the English Midlands has successfully revived a waterway that had been heavily modified for agricultural purposes over 150 years. Led by the Trent R...

item.title: Goss acknowledges tough competition for Bafana Bafana World Cup squad
item.description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaching, Bafana Bafana's goalkeeper selection is shaping up to be highly competitive. Ricardo Goss, currently on loan at Siwelele from Mamelodi Sundowns, has acknowled...

item.title: US Military Loses 42 Aircraft, Including Drones and Fighter Jets, in Iran Conflict
item.description: According to a recent study by the US Congressional Research Service, the United States lost or had damaged at least 42 military aircraft during its operations in Iran. The list includes four F-15E St...
